label: extract fallback chain v2 (local is the main path)
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal editorial mini-comic illustration that explains ONE idea: "extract tries the fastest reader first (local defuddle) — that is the default and usually lands clean; behind it sits a fallback chain that only wakes if the rung above missed."

Composition (mini-comic, 4 panels in ONE horizontal row, read left to right, separated by clear thin hand-drawn panel borders with clean gutters): The SAME mascot (Blot the ink-droplet) appears in every panel, identical design and palette. The key object is ONE document page that starts messy with navigation chrome and ends clean. One action per panel. The first panel is the HAPPY MAIN PATH, drawn largest and most confident; panels 2–4 are the FALLBACK CHAIN that only fires on miss, slightly smaller and calmer, each with a tiny "if above missed" cue. Generous negative space inside each panel.
  Panel 1 (the main path, the default) — Blot feeds the messy chrome-laden page into a small hand-drawn machine labelled "local"; a CLEAN page comes straight out the other side, chrome gone; a bold ✓ mark. This is the normal outcome.
  Panel 2 (fallback rung 1) — the same Blot, the same page, feeds it into a machine labelled "jina"; small "local missed" cue above; the page comes out clean; a ✓ mark but quieter than panel 1.
  Panel 3 (fallback rung 2) — same Blot, same page, machine labelled "defuddle.md"; "local & jina missed" cue above; page comes out clean; ✓.
  Panel 4 (last resort) — same Blot, same page, machine labelled "API"; "all readers missed" cue above; the page comes out WITH the chrome still on it (the old dirty path); a small ✗ — this is the fallback of last resort, not a goal.
The visual logic: panel 1 is the big confident win; panels 2–4 shrink slightly and read as a safety net below it — the chain exists, but local is what you usually get. Every reader EXCEPT the API returns a clean page; the API alone returns the messy page, marking it as the degraded last resort.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot — a plump rounded ink-droplet body (a fat soft teardrop, wide at the bottom, narrowing to a gently curved tip at the top), two simple dot eyes, blank deadpan (no eyebrows, no mouth), small stubby arms and legs; the ONLY accent-colored part is the droplet tip. It MUST perform the move, not decorate. In this dark palette the body is filled with the structure ink and the eyes are warm-white. The mascot is a solid OPAQUE shape in front of the scene — no ground line, table edge, horizon, or prop passes through its body; background lines stop at its silhouette. Its limbs join the body cleanly at sensible points; only the mascot's own parts touch its outline. Hold at most one prop per hand; the page is HELD in the hands and clearly separated from the torso. Preserve the character sheet's limb proportions: a stubby arm stays stubby and nearby, never stretched across a whole panel.

LINE LANGUAGE: draw EVERYTHING — mascot, page, machines, ✓/✗ marks — in ONE bold, even-weight, softly-rounded outline (clean vinyl-sticker line), not thin scratchy sketch lines.

STYLE: risograph print — grainy halftone texture, slight ink-layer offset, faint paper grain, flat fills, no gradients, no soft shadows.

PALETTE: paper #fffef7. Structure ink #111111 for all linework, forms, and text. Accent #ff3d9a used sparingly — the character's droplet tip in every panel, plus the bold ✓ in panel 1 (the main-win marker is the one accent element beyond the character).

TEXT HIERARCHY: hand-letter exactly 4 short labels in structure ink on bare paper, one per panel, each placed near its machine: "local ✓" (panel 1, largest/most confident), "jina ✓" (panel 2), "defuddle.md ✓" (panel 3), "API ✗" (panel 4). Keep the panel-1 label visually primary; panels 2–4 labels smaller and subordinate. Each label appears EXACTLY ONCE. Never put text on a colored fill. No title bar, no type label, no logo, no extra words, no numbers.

Aspect ratio: 16:9.
